authentication.provider_portal: OAuth 2.0 with provider credentialing verification. MFA required for all provider users. Session timeout: 15 minutes idle, 8 hours absolute.
authentication.clinical_reviewer: SAML 2.0 SSO via payer identity provider. MFA required. Role-based access per LOB assignment.
authentication.system_to_system: mTLS with X.509 certificates for all service-to-service communication. Certificate rotation: 90-day automated renewal via AWS ACM.
authentication.clinical_data: SMART on FHIR (OAuth 2.0) for FHIR R4 endpoint access. Scoped tokens per FHIR resource type. 15-minute token expiry.
authentication.admin: Privileged access management (PAM) for LOB configuration changes. Require two-person approval for production config changes.

secrets_management.strategy: AWS Secrets Manager for all credentials, API keys, and certificates. No secrets in code, environment variables, or configuration files.
secrets_management.rotation: Automated 90-day rotation for all service account credentials. Immediate rotation upon personnel change.
secrets_management.audit: All secret access logged to CloudTrail. Anomalous access patterns trigger SNS alerts.

network_isolation.tiers: Three-tier network: Public (ALB, API Gateway, WAF), Private (ECS services, Lambda, Kafka), Isolated (RDS, DynamoDB, HealthLake, S3).
network_isolation.cross_vpc: AWS PrivateLink for all cross-VPC connectivity to Facets and legacy clinical databases. No public internet exposure for PHI data flows.
network_isolation.external: Autonomize AI Genesis Platform accessed via PrivateLink or site-to-site VPN. All PHI encrypted before crossing network boundaries.
network_isolation.segmentation: Security groups per component with deny-all-default. NACLs on subnet boundaries. VPC flow logs enabled on all subnets.

ai_guardrails.content_filtering: All AI model outputs validated for PHI leakage before delivery. Clinical content filtered for accuracy markers.
ai_guardrails.pii_detection: Amazon Comprehend PII detection on all model inputs and outputs. PHI classified per HIPAA 18 identifiers.
ai_guardrails.prompt_injection: Input sanitization on all user-provided clinical text. System prompts isolated from user content. Output format validation.
ai_guardrails.model_access: Each AI agent has scoped IAM role. No cross-agent data access. All model invocations logged with request/response hashes.

compliance.data_classification: All PA data classified as PHI (Restricted). De-identified data for analytics classified as Internal.
compliance.data_residency: All PHI must reside in US-based AWS regions (us-east-1, us-west-2). No cross-border PHI transfer.

threat_model.scope: AI-driven Prior Authorization system for large US health plan — all components from PA request ingestion through determination delivery, including Autonomize AI platform integration, clinical data access, and MLOps pipeline.

threats.description: Unauthorized entity submits PA request via provider portal using stolen OAuth credentials, gaining access to member PHI and potentially approving unauthorized services.
threats.mitigation: MFA required for all provider portal access. Device fingerprinting and IP geolocation anomaly detection. Provider NPI (National Provider Identifier) verification against NPPES registry on every submission. Rate limiting per provider account.

threats.description: Compromised service account credentials used to access TriZetto Facets member enrollment data, enabling mass PHI exfiltration or eligibility manipulation.
threats.mitigation: Service accounts stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day automated rotation. Facets access scoped to read-only eligibility queries (no write). All Facets API calls logged to CloudTrail with anomaly alerting. PrivateLink ensures no internet exposure.

threats.description: Rogue FHIR endpoint injected into clinical data federation returns falsified clinical records, leading to incorrect PA determinations.
threats.mitigation: FHIR endpoint registry with pre-approved endpoints only (whitelist). SMART on FHIR mutual authentication with certificate pinning. FHIR Bundle signature verification (Provenance resource). New endpoints require security review before activation.

threats.description: Prompt injection via crafted clinical notes in PA request manipulates AI decision agent to approve requests that should be denied or pended for clinical review.
threats.mitigation: Input sanitization pipeline strips control characters and injection patterns from clinical text before AI processing. System prompts isolated in separate context window from user-provided content. Output validation: all AI determinations cross-checked against deterministic rules engine before finalization. Confidence score < 0.85 forces human review regardless of AI output.

threats.description: Training data poisoning via manipulated reviewer corrections — adversary submitting systematically biased corrections to shift model behavior toward inappropriate approvals.
threats.mitigation: Reviewer corrections validated against clinical guidelines before inclusion in training data. Statistical anomaly detection on correction patterns per reviewer (flag >2 standard deviations from baseline). Two-reviewer validation for corrections that contradict guidelines. Monthly model evaluation against held-out golden dataset before production promotion.

threats.description: Modification of PA determination in transit between Autonomize AI platform and Kafka event bus, changing approval to denial or vice versa.
threats.mitigation: All Kafka messages signed with HMAC-SHA256 using per-producer keys. Message integrity verified by consumers before processing. mTLS on all Kafka broker connections. PrivateLink for Autonomize AI connectivity — no internet traversal. Determination hash stored in audit trail for tamper detection.

threats.description: Unauthorized modification of LOB configuration rules (e.g., lowering confidence threshold from 0.85 to 0.50) to increase auto-approval rate without clinical validation.
threats.mitigation: All configuration changes require two-person approval via PAM workflow. Configuration stored in DynamoDB with version history and immutable change log. Critical thresholds (confidence, auto-determination eligibility) have guardrail ranges that cannot be exceeded. Production config changes trigger SNS alert to compliance team.

threats.description: AI-generated PA determination cannot be reconstructed for regulatory inquiry — no traceable chain from input data to clinical rationale to final decision.
threats.mitigation: Immutable audit trail in DynamoDB captures complete decision chain: input documents (S3 refs), clinical data accessed (FHIR Bundle refs), guidelines applied (InterQual/MCG criteria IDs), AI confidence score, model version, human reviewer ID (if applicable), final determination with rationale. Cryptographic hash chain links audit records. 7-year retention per HIPAA. Audit records replicated to separate AWS account with read-only cross-account access.

threats.description: Clinical reviewer denies making a determination decision, claiming system error or unauthorized access to their account.
threats.mitigation: SAML 2.0 SSO with MFA produces cryptographically verifiable authentication assertion. Session activity logged with IP, device fingerprint, and timestamp. Reviewer actions require explicit confirmation click with session token verification. All reviewer decisions timestamped and linked to authenticated session ID in audit trail.

threats.description: PHI leakage through application logs — clinical data, member identifiers, or diagnosis codes written to CloudWatch Logs in plaintext.
threats.mitigation: Structured JSON logging with PHI field redaction enforced at application layer. Log sanitization Lambda function strips 18 HIPAA identifiers before CloudWatch ingestion. Log access restricted to security team IAM role. CloudWatch Logs encrypted with KMS customer-managed key. Automated PHI detection scan on log groups (weekly) with alert on violations.

threats.description: Cross-LOB data leakage in multi-tenant architecture — Commercial LOB user accesses Medicare Advantage patient records through shared Kafka topics or FHIR Facade.
threats.mitigation: Kafka topics partitioned by LOB ID with consumer group ACLs enforcing LOB isolation. FHIR Facade queries include mandatory LOB filter (tenant ID) in all clinical data requests. Row-level security in PostgreSQL keyed on LOB ID. DynamoDB partition key includes LOB prefix. QuickSight dashboard row-level security per user LOB assignment. Penetration test for cross-tenant access before go-live.

threats.description: AI model output inadvertently includes PHI from other patients seen during training — model memorization of sensitive data from training corpus.
threats.mitigation: Training data de-identified before model fine-tuning (Safe Harbor method). Model output validation scans for PHI patterns (SSN, MRN, DOB, names) before delivery. Differential privacy techniques applied during fine-tuning. Periodic model probing tests for memorization of training data. Autonomize AI platform's built-in content guardrails provide additional PHI filtering layer.

threats.description: Fax documents containing PHI stored unencrypted in S3 staging bucket, accessible to unauthorized IAM roles.
threats.mitigation: S3 bucket policy enforces SSE-KMS encryption on all objects (deny unencrypted uploads). S3 bucket is private (no public access), with Block Public Access enabled. IAM policies restrict access to Document Processing Lambda role only. S3 access logging enabled. Lifecycle policy moves processed faxes to S3 Glacier after 90 days. S3 Object Lock for compliance retention.

threats.description: Autonomize AI Genesis Platform outage blocks all AI-driven PA determinations, creating backlog of 80,000+ daily requests with no automated processing.
threats.mitigation: Graceful degradation: if Autonomize AI platform is unavailable, system automatically routes all PA requests to human review queue via Step Functions fallback workflow. Kafka retains events for replay when platform recovers. Health check on Autonomize AI endpoint every 30 seconds. SLA requirement in Autonomize AI contract: 99.9% availability. Alerting: PagerDuty escalation if platform unavailable > 5 minutes.

threats.description: AWS Textract API rate limiting under high fax volume prevents OCR processing, causing fax ingestion backlog.
threats.mitigation: Textract async API with S3 output (not synchronous). SQS queue between S3 events and Textract invocations for rate smoothing. Exponential backoff with jitter on throttled requests. AWS service quota increase request for Textract in us-east-1. Monitor Textract usage vs. quota in CloudWatch.

threats.description: DDoS attack on provider portal API Gateway overwhelms ingestion pipeline with fraudulent PA requests.
threats.mitigation: AWS WAF with rate limiting rules (100 requests/minute per provider IP). AWS Shield Advanced for DDoS protection. API Gateway throttling at 1,000 RPS with burst to 2,500. CloudFront distribution for geographic filtering. Bot detection rules in WAF. Anomaly-based detection triggers automated IP blocking.

threats.description: Cross-LOB privilege escalation — user assigned to Commercial LOB accesses Medicare Advantage PA data through API parameter manipulation or direct database query.
threats.mitigation: LOB authorization enforced at API Gateway level (JWT claims include LOB scope). Server-side LOB filter injected into all database queries — no client-side LOB selection. Kafka consumer groups scoped to LOB partitions. FHIR Facade validates LOB authorization before clinical data retrieval. Quarterly access review audit. Penetration testing for cross-tenant bypass.

threats.description: AI Decision Agent jailbreaked to bypass clinical guidelines — crafted input causes agent to ignore InterQual/MCG criteria and approve request based solely on AI reasoning.
threats.mitigation: Deterministic guardrail: all AI-approved determinations must have corresponding guideline match from InterQual/MCG API — no approval without positive guidelines match. Decision Agent output validated against rules engine before finalization. Any determination that bypasses guidelines matching is automatically pended for human review. Model behavior monitoring detects guideline bypass patterns.

layer_1_network_perimeter.description: Three-tier network segmentation with defense at every boundary
controls: Public tier: ALB + API Gateway + AWS WAF + AWS Shield Advanced — only HTTPS (443) exposed
Private tier: ECS Fargate services, Lambda, MSK — no internet access, NAT Gateway for outbound-only
Isolated tier: RDS PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, HealthLake, S3 — VPC endpoints only, no NAT
Cross-VPC: AWS PrivateLink for Facets, legacy DBs, and Autonomize AI — no public internet
VPC Flow Logs enabled on all subnets, forwarded to CloudWatch Logs for analysis
Security groups: deny-all-default, allowlist per component with minimum required ports
NACLs: subnet-level stateless filtering as defense-in-depth behind security groups

layer_2_identity_and_access.description: Dedicated identity per service/user with least-privilege enforcement
controls: Provider users: OAuth 2.0 + MFA via payer identity provider. NPI verification.
Clinical reviewers: SAML 2.0 SSO + MFA. Role-based access per LOB assignment.
Service accounts: Per-component IAM roles (no shared roles). STS AssumeRole with session tags for LOB context.
AI agents: Each Autonomize AI agent has scoped IAM role — Document Agent cannot access Guidelines API, etc.
Admin access: PAM with two-person approval. Break-glass procedures for emergency access with post-incident review.
SMART on FHIR: Per-EMR OAuth 2.0 with scoped tokens (Patient/*.read only). 15-minute token expiry.
Certificate management: AWS ACM for TLS certificates. 90-day rotation. Certificate pinning for FHIR endpoints.

layer_3_application_security.description: Input validation, output sanitization, and AI-specific guardrails
controls: Input validation: All PA request fields validated against schema before processing
X12 278 parsing: Strict ASC X12 grammar validation — reject malformed transactions
PHI detection: Amazon Comprehend PII detection on all inputs before AI processing
Prompt injection prevention: Clinical text sanitized, system prompts isolated, output format validated
AI output validation: Determinations cross-checked against deterministic rules engine
Content Security Policy: API responses include CSP headers, CORS restricted to allowed origins
OWASP Top 10: SQL injection prevention (parameterized queries), XSS prevention, CSRF tokens

layer_4_data_protection.description: Encrypt everything, everywhere — at rest, in transit, and in processing
controls: At rest: AES-256 via AWS KMS customer-managed keys (CMK) — S3 SSE-KMS, RDS encryption, DynamoDB encryption, HealthLake encryption
In transit: TLS 1.2+ on all connections — mTLS for service-to-service, SMART on FHIR for clinical data
PHI field-level: Sensitive PHI fields (SSN, MRN, DOB) encrypted with separate KMS key for field-level access control
Key management: AWS KMS with automatic annual key rotation. Key policy restricts usage to authorized roles only.
Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day automated rotation. Secrets never in code or environment variables.
Backup encryption: RDS automated backups encrypted. S3 cross-region replication uses destination KMS key.
Data masking: PHI masked in logs, dashboards, and non-production environments. Synthetic data for dev/test.

layer_5_monitoring_and_audit.description: Comprehensive logging, anomaly detection, and incident response
controls: CloudTrail: All AWS API calls logged. Multi-region trail with S3 log file validation.
VPC Flow Logs: All network traffic logged for forensic analysis.
Application logs: Structured JSON with correlation ID. PHI redacted. 30-day hot, 7-year cold retention.
Audit trail: Immutable DynamoDB records with cryptographic hash chain. Separate audit AWS account.
Anomaly detection: CloudWatch anomaly detection on API call patterns, Kafka throughput, model confidence distribution.
Alerting: SNS → PagerDuty for critical security events (unauthorized access, PHI detection in logs, configuration changes).
Incident response: Security incident response runbook. 1-hour notification for HIPAA breach assessment. CIRT on-call rotation.
SIEM integration: CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs + application logs forwarded to payer SIEM for correlation.

api_gateway_role.permissions: Execute Lambda functions (ingestion, validation). Read WAF rules. Write CloudWatch Logs.
api_gateway_role.scope: Function ARN scoped. No S3, no database access.

document_processing_role.permissions: Read S3 fax bucket. Invoke Textract (StartDocumentAnalysis, GetDocumentAnalysis). Invoke Comprehend Medical (DetectEntitiesV2). Write to Kafka (pa-requests topic).
document_processing_role.scope: S3 bucket ARN scoped. Textract/Comprehend in us-east-1 only.

fhir_facade_role.permissions: Read HealthLake FHIR store. Invoke SMART on FHIR endpoints (Patient/*.read, Condition/*.read, Observation/*.read). Read legacy DBs via JDBC (PrivateLink). Write to Kafka (clinical-data topic).
fhir_facade_role.scope: HealthLake ARN scoped. FHIR read-only. Legacy DB read-only.

eligibility_service_role.permissions: Read Secrets Manager (Facets credentials). Invoke Facets API (eligibility endpoint only). Read/write DynamoDB DAX (eligibility cache). Write to Kafka (eligibility-results topic).
eligibility_service_role.scope: Facets API scoped to eligibility queries. No member enrollment write access.

pa_copilot_connector_role.permissions: Read/write Kafka (pa-requests, pa-enriched, pa-determinations topics). Invoke Autonomize AI Genesis API via PrivateLink. Read DynamoDB LOB configuration.
pa_copilot_connector_role.scope: Kafka topic ARN scoped. No direct S3 or database access.

audit_service_role.permissions: Read Kafka (audit-events topic). Write DynamoDB (audit table — append-only, no update/delete). Write S3 (audit archive bucket).
audit_service_role.scope: DynamoDB table scoped. S3 bucket scoped. No read access to PHI data stores.

mlops_pipeline_role.permissions: Read DynamoDB (reviewer corrections). Read/write S3 (training data, model artifacts). Invoke SageMaker (Processing, Training, Endpoints). Read CloudWatch (model metrics).
mlops_pipeline_role.scope: S3 prefix scoped to mlops/. SageMaker in us-east-1. Training data must be de-identified.

config_admin_role.permissions: Read/write DynamoDB (configuration table). Two-person approval required via PAM. All changes logged to audit trail.
config_admin_role.scope: Configuration table only. No access to PA data, clinical data, or audit records.

authentication.mfa: Required for all human users. TOTP or hardware security key.
authentication.session: 15-minute idle timeout. 8-hour absolute timeout. JWT with RS256 signing.

authorization.model: RBAC with LOB-level data isolation enforced at API Gateway and database layers
authorization.enforcement: Server-side LOB filter injected on all queries. No client-side LOB selection permitted.

network.vpc_cidr: 10.0.0.0/16 with /24 subnets per AZ per tier
network.public_subnets: ALB and API Gateway only. No direct EC2/ECS in public tier.
network.private_subnets: ECS Fargate, Lambda, MSK. NAT Gateway for outbound (e.g., Textract API).
network.isolated_subnets: RDS, DynamoDB VPC endpoints, HealthLake, S3 VPC endpoints. No internet route.
network.cross_vpc: PrivateLink to Facets, legacy DBs, Autonomize AI. No VPC peering (more granular control).

encryption.at_rest: AES-256 via KMS CMK. Separate keys for: PHI data, audit data, ML artifacts.
encryption.in_transit: TLS 1.2+ everywhere. mTLS for service-to-service. SMART on FHIR for clinical data.
encryption.field_level: PHI identifiers (SSN, MRN, DOB, names) encrypted with dedicated KMS key. Application-layer decryption only when clinically necessary.

security_architecture.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day automated rotation. No secrets in code, config files, or environment variables. Secret access logged to CloudTrail.

logging_and_monitoring.centralized_logging: CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON. PHI redacted at application layer.
logging_and_monitoring.security_monitoring: CloudTrail for API audit. GuardDuty for threat detection. Security Hub for compliance posture.
logging_and_monitoring.incident_response: 1-hour HIPAA breach assessment notification. Security incident runbook. CIRT on-call.

prompt_injection_protection: Input sanitization: strip control characters, injection patterns, and encoded payloads from clinical text
System prompt isolation: Autonomize AI agents use separated system/user context windows
Output format validation: all AI outputs must match expected JSON schema — reject free-form outputs
Deterministic guardrail: AI approval requires positive guideline match from InterQual/MCG — cannot approve on reasoning alone
Confidence threshold: any determination below 0.85 confidence automatically routes to human review
output_filtering: PHI scan on all AI model outputs using Comprehend PII detection before delivery
Clinical accuracy markers validated against guideline citations
Response schema validation — determination must include required fields (rationale, guideline IDs, confidence)
Cross-patient data leak detection — verify output PHI matches input patient only
ai_security_controls.model_access_control: Each Autonomize AI agent has scoped IAM role. Model invocation logged with request hash, response hash, model version, latency. Rate limiting per agent. API key rotation every 90 days.
ai_security_controls.data_poisoning_prevention: Training data de-identified (Safe Harbor). Reviewer correction anomaly detection (statistical). Two-reviewer validation for guideline-contradicting corrections. Golden dataset holdout for monthly model evaluation. Model version rollback capability.
ai_security_controls.hallucination_mitigation: All clinical determinations grounded in specific InterQual/MCG guideline citations. Guideline IDs must resolve to actual published criteria. Deterministic cross-check: AI approval must have matching positive guideline result from InterQual/MCG API call. Hallucinated guideline citations flagged by validation service.

compliance_mapping.requirement: 164.312(a)(1) — Access Control: Unique user identification, emergency access, automatic logoff, encryption/decryption
compliance_mapping.evidence: OAuth 2.0/SAML 2.0 with unique user IDs. MFA enforced. 15-min idle timeout. AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit. Break-glass emergency access with post-incident audit.

compliance_mapping.requirement: 164.312(c)(1) — Integrity Controls: Mechanism to authenticate ePHI, protect from improper alteration/destruction
compliance_mapping.evidence: Kafka message signing (HMAC-SHA256). DynamoDB audit trail with cryptographic hash chain. S3 Object Lock for document immutability. Database transaction logging.

compliance_mapping.requirement: 164.312(e)(1) — Transmission Security: Encryption of ePHI in transit, integrity controls
compliance_mapping.evidence: TLS 1.2+ on all connections. mTLS for service-to-service. PrivateLink for cross-VPC (no internet traversal). SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0 for clinical data.

compliance_mapping.requirement: 164.312(b) — Audit Controls: Record and examine activity in systems containing ePHI
compliance_mapping.evidence: CloudTrail for AWS API audit. Immutable DynamoDB audit trail for PA decisions. VPC Flow Logs. Application-level structured logging with correlation IDs. 7-year retention.

compliance_mapping.requirement: Minimum Necessary Standard — limit PHI access to minimum necessary for intended purpose
compliance_mapping.evidence: Least-privilege IAM roles per component. FHIR scoped tokens (Patient/*.read only). PHI field-level encryption with dedicated KMS key. AI agents scoped to specific data access patterns.

compliance_mapping.requirement: Breach notification within 60 days of discovery; immediate assessment within 1 hour
compliance_mapping.evidence: Security incident response runbook. GuardDuty threat detection. CloudWatch anomaly alerting. 1-hour breach assessment notification.
compliance_mapping.notes: Breach response plan documented but not yet tested via tabletop exercise. Recommendation: conduct breach simulation before go-live.

compliance_mapping.requirement: Right to know, delete, opt-out for California member data
compliance_mapping.evidence: Data inventory includes California member identification via Facets benefit plan. Deletion workflow designed but not yet implemented. Opt-out mechanism for non-essential data processing in development.
compliance_mapping.notes: CCPA rights management is Phase 2 scope. Phase 1 ensures data inventory and tagging supports future CCPA requests.

compliance_mapping.requirement: FHIR R4 Prior Authorization API with specific denial reasons, 72-hour urgent turnaround
compliance_mapping.evidence: FHIR R4 PA API per HL7 Da Vinci PAS Implementation Guide (FR-008). Specific denial reason codes included in all denial responses. Auto-determined cases target < 24-hour turnaround (exceeds 72-hour requirement).
compliance_mapping.notes: Compliance deadline: January 1, 2027. Architecture supports full compliance; implementation in 12-week roadmap.

compliance_mapping.requirement: Real-time PA status inquiry via FHIR API
compliance_mapping.evidence: FHIR R4 PA status endpoint returns real-time status. Kafka event sourcing provides current PA state at any point. API Gateway exposes status endpoint per Da Vinci PAS IG.

compliance_mapping.requirement: SMART on FHIR authorization for clinical data access
compliance_mapping.evidence: FHIR R4 Facade (C-005) implements SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0 for all FHIR endpoint access. Scoped tokens per resource type. 15-minute expiry.

compliance_mapping.requirement: Third-party AI platform must maintain SOC 2 Type 2 certification
compliance_mapping.evidence: Autonomize AI holds SOC 2 Type 2 certification (verified from company materials). Annual audit report review required as part of vendor management.
compliance_mapping.notes: Require annual SOC 2 report review and right-to-audit clause in contract.

must: Enforce least-privilege IAM on all service accounts — no wildcard permissions
Store all secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with automated 90-day rotation
Enable audit logging on all sensitive operations (CloudTrail, DynamoDB audit, VPC Flow Logs)
Encrypt all PHI at rest (AES-256 KMS) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
Validate all AI model inputs for prompt injection and PHI patterns before processing
Cross-check all AI determinations against deterministic rules engine before finalization
Enforce LOB tenant isolation at API Gateway, database, and Kafka layers
Conduct HIPAA breach response tabletop exercise before go-live
Require MFA for all human users (providers, reviewers, admins)
Maintain immutable audit trail with 7-year retention for all PA decisions
De-identify training data before model fine-tuning (Safe Harbor method)
Conduct penetration test focusing on cross-tenant and prompt injection before go-live
must_not: Never store secrets in code, config files, environment variables, or container images
Never expose PHI in application logs — enforce redaction at application layer
Never allow AI to auto-deny PA requests in Phase 1 — auto-approval only
Never permit direct internet access from isolated database subnets
Never use shared service accounts across components — one IAM role per service
Never deploy without security testing (SAST, DAST, dependency scanning)
Never use production PHI data in development or testing environments
Never bypass two-person approval for production configuration changes
Never allow client-side LOB selection — server-side enforcement only
Never process PA requests without eligibility verification

open_items.finding: HIPAA breach response plan not yet validated through tabletop exercise
open_items.recommendation: Conduct breach simulation tabletop exercise during QA/UAT phase (Week 9-10). Include: 1-hour assessment workflow, breach notification templates, CIRT escalation, HHS reporting procedures.

open_items.finding: Penetration testing not yet scheduled for cross-tenant isolation and AI prompt injection
open_items.recommendation: Engage third-party pen test firm for pre-go-live security assessment. Scope: cross-LOB tenant isolation, prompt injection attacks on PA Copilot, API Gateway bypass, FHIR Facade authorization bypass.

open_items.finding: PHI log redaction relies on application-layer enforcement — no automated verification that redaction is working
open_items.recommendation: Implement weekly automated PHI scan on CloudWatch log groups using Macie or custom Comprehend PII detection Lambda. Alert on any PHI detected in logs. Include in monitoring runbook.

open_items.finding: CCPA rights management (deletion, opt-out) not implemented in Phase 1
open_items.recommendation: Ensure data tagging infrastructure supports future CCPA requests. Implement deletion workflow in Phase 2. Document gap in compliance posture with timeline.

open_items.finding: Autonomize AI contract must include right-to-audit clause and annual SOC 2 report review
open_items.recommendation: Legal team to negotiate: right-to-audit, annual SOC 2 report delivery, HIPAA BAA (Business Associate Agreement), breach notification obligations, data handling upon contract termination.

change_impact.recommendation: New AI-driven system processing PHI at enterprise scale. HIGH risk due to: (1) PHI exposure surface across 14 components, (2) AI decision-making with patient care implications, (3) multi-tenant data isolation requirements, (4) regulatory compliance (HIPAA, CMS-0057-F). Require: pre-go-live pen test, breach tabletop exercise, HIPAA BAA with Autonomize AI, and phased LOB rollout starting with lowest-risk LOB.
